ESCALATE_INCIDENT.definition: An open support record changes from its current support tier to a numerically higher support tier, transferring responsibility to that tier while remaining open.
positive_examples: INC-1047 moved from Tier 1 ownership to Tier 2 ownership and remained open.
negative_examples: INC-1047 changed owning team while its support tier stayed fixed.

REASSIGN_INCIDENT.definition: An open support record changes owning team while its support tier remains fixed.
positive_examples: INC-1047 changed ownership from Service Desk to Billing while staying at Tier 1.
negative_examples: INC-1047 moved from Tier 1 to Tier 2 while keeping the same owning team.

CLOSE_INCIDENT.definition: An open support record with complete information leaves the active work queue because handling is finished.
positive_examples: INC-2450 had complete information, left the open queue, and was marked finished.
negative_examples: INC-2450 remained open while the reporter was asked for more details.

REQUEST_MORE_INFORMATION.definition: An agent adds a specified public message to an open support record, notifies its original reporter, and pauses work awaiting that reporter's response while the current support team retains ownership.
positive_examples: A public message specifying the missing logs was added to INC-3120, its original reporter was notified, and the service desk retained ownership while waiting for a response.
negative_examples: INC-3120 was merely marked as waiting without a reporter-facing message or notification.
INC-3120 was reassigned to another support team without asking its reporter for anything.

REQUEST_APPROVAL.definition: A pending authorization item is sent to a named role so that role can authorize or deny it before work proceeds.
positive_examples: APR-0021 was sent to a manager for authorization and no work proceeded yet.
negative_examples: An order charge issue was sent to a manager for examination rather than authorization.

REFUND_DUPLICATE_CHARGE.definition: A confirmed second charge below 500 USD is returned to the customer for the affected order.
positive_examples: The confirmed second 120 USD charge on ORD-0077 was returned to the customer.
negative_examples: The charge on ORD-0077 was disputed and sent to a manager without returning money.

REQUEST_MANAGER_REVIEW.definition: An order charge issue is sent to a manager for examination instead of acting on the charge directly.
positive_examples: ORD-0912 was sent to a manager for examination before any money was returned.
negative_examples: The confirmed second charge on ORD-0077 was returned directly to the customer.

SUSPEND_ACCOUNT.definition: An active account is prevented from normal use because of fraud, abuse, or non-payment.
positive_examples: ACC-4002 was prevented from transacting after fraud was confirmed.
negative_examples: A related support record was finished while ACC-4002 remained active.
